mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 3.5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 3.5% show negative or neutral performance (mean < 0.1% with p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in industrial production and housing starts indicates expanding real economic activity, which drives equity risk premiums higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns show no significant correlation with combined INDPRO/HOUST growth (rolling window IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP growth below 2% signals deteriorating demand conditions that trigger risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in GDPC1 < 2% periods show positive or neutral performance (mean > -0.2% with p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in CPI inflation above 3% prompts expectations of tighter monetary policy, which drives equity valuations lower and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in CPIAUCSL > 3% periods show positive or neutral performance (mean > -0.1% with p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 200k signals strong labor market dynamics that boost consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in PAYEMS > 200k periods show negative or neutral performance (mean < 0.05% with p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in real sales of new homes below 1.2 million units indicates weakening demand conditions that drive equity market risk-off sentiment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in HOUST < 1.2M periods show positive or neutral performance (mean > -0.15% with p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in consumer credit and retail sales signals expanding private demand, which drives equity earnings expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns show no significant correlation with combined RSAFS/CPIAUCSL growth (rolling window IC < 0.05).
